what_it_does: Synthesizes three design-grounding investigations into a single self-build substrate plan, reconciles it with an existing substrate seed, identifies blockers, proposes decisions for Tim, and maps what to reuse versus build.
observations: The file is dated 2026-06-17 and explicitly provisional, awaiting Tim's decisions.
It converges on DNA's already-built substrate rather than building a parallel coverage/forms/lifter system.
It extends the substrate concept into three pillars: SEE (coverage), SHAPE (forms + lifters), and ACT (triggers).
It reports that forms, lifters, and trigger links are built and tested but not called by any working path.
The central blocker is identified as Python-callable recognisers, which the shared auto-authoring gate rejects.
It recommends moving recognisers from code to data using RULE_OPS shape-ops and regex strings.
It proposes a shared global/project/user scope base for multiple registries.
It recommends regex-based TS/JS extractors now and flags tree-sitter-typescript as the later upgrade.
It recommends a built-but-dormant trigger driver, armed only when Tim decides.
It suggests optional capture→forms and lifter lanes with by_form=False default to keep current byte-identical behavior.
standouts: The file reframes the build as mostly connecting and hardening existing mechanisms rather than net-new construction.
It diagnoses one shared root cause — callable recognisers — that blocks forms, lifters, and triggers from being agent-authorable.
It positions DNA's substrate as the seed/reference while the company engine becomes the canonical generalization.
It insists on a built-not-armed trigger driver to avoid an always-on daemon.
conventions: Uses star-marked sections for decisions and lead-decided recommendations.
References external board items and counterpart documents by path/ID.
Uses an uppercase, heavily emphasized heading style.
Organizes content as decision/recommendation lists plus reuse/build/sequence maps.
Frames the work through the SEE/SHAPE/ACT conceptual triad.
concerns: The design is provisional and contains multiple explicit decisions still awaiting Tim.
The 80/20 connecting-vs-net-new estimate is asserted without supporting metrics.
Several items are marked 'lead-decided' and may still need approval.
The file mixes architectural strategy, implementation sequencing, and policy in one document.
It assumes a shared auto-authoring gate rule that is not detailed inside this file.
notes: A pre-builder design synthesis arguing that the real unlock is converting recognisers from code to data so existing substrate pieces can be wired together.
questions: What are the exact schemas and contents of the three referenced design docs?
How does the shared auto-authoring gate enforce the no-callable-fields rule?
Where exactly are DNA's substrate engine files located and what is their current state?
What criteria will Tim use to approve the collapsed decisions?
How is 'project' scope resolved when no active-project singleton exists?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to synthesize three design investigations into the plan Tim asked for before builders launch; the file does that and additionally reconciles the plan with DNA's existing substrate and makes convergence/placement recommendations.
intention_signals: It is trying to align the company's self-build tooling with an existing substrate seed, remove the authoring bottleneck by turning recognisers into data, and produce an actionable, approval-ready build sequence.
novelty: The explicit SEE/SHAPE/ACT triad and the diagnosis that a single shared serialization gate rejecting callable recognisers is the root blocker, with one data-recogniser move unblocking all three pillars.
contribution: It provides a decision-ready synthesis that maps existing substrate work onto the company's scope, identifies the recogniser-to-data conversion as the unblocking foundation, and supplies a reuse/build/sequence plan for the trigger and coverage layers.
summary_for_embedding: A 2026-06-17 design synthesis for the company's self-build substrate that converges on DNA's existing substrate seed, extends it with an ACT/trigger layer and a company-wide engine, and argues that moving recognisers from code to data is the central unlock.
intention_for_embedding: To give Tim a pre-builder decision document reconciling three design investigations with an existing substrate and defining the build sequence for coverage, forms, lifters, and triggers.
